title: Brahma Sutra
edition_note: Pilot passages and grader notes only. Verify against the edition and commentary tradition you choose before public release.

passages.grader_use: Use for grading answers about the beginning of Vedanta inquiry and its contrast with ritual inquiry.

passages.translation_or_note: Brahman is that from which the origin and so on of this world proceed.
Because scripture is the source or means of knowledge.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading questions about scripture as pramana for Brahman.

passages.translation_or_note: But that is known from the coherent purport of scripture.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading samanvaya and Upanishadic purport questions.

passages.text: Some Vedanta pedagogy uses provisional attribution followed by negation or refinement to guide the student beyond mistaken literalization of preliminary descriptions.
passages.translation_or_note: This is a compact grader note, not a Brahma Sutra quotation.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading questions on superimposition and rescission as teaching method.
